body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 

This is a large refactor of CTS, which  resolves a number of longstanding issues:

* Debugs multirate methods by correcting mismatched `tstops` for inner and outer integrators of multirate methods, which led them to fail; updates tests to include them and verify their convergence: Commit f1534a1
* Removes `KernelAbstractions` dependency (was unnecessary anyway, broadcasts can handle what it did) and implements local callbacks, to replace `SciML` callbacks. Closes #308: Commit 9f879da
* Removes `SciMLBase` and `DiffEqBase` dependency; simplifies API and makes type structure flatter: Commit 421b33e
* Simplifies ClimaODEFunction and decomposes IMEX ARK update_stage! into more modular and readable blocks: Commit 4dd6168
* Cleanup of code and updates of docstrings and existing docs to be consistent in style and with code: Commit ab9f320
* Added new landing page for docs and new tutorials; docs cleanup; removed unnecessary dependencies; new logo. Closes #267: Commit 4290f30
* Cleanup. Added AD tests and docs/tutorial, downstream tests: Commit 7554a6c

Closes #393 . (Though Newton solve refactor remains to be considered/done. Moved to issue #399.)

## Reviewer Guide

I am aware that this is a long PR. To review, one can either do this commit-by-commit, as outlined above, or in one block. It's probably best to read the built docs in their totality, since they are almost entirely changed. 

It's easier to have one PR, rather than several, because the various commits interact, and review changes in one would affect others, so are easier to do in one go. (Also: it's what happens when review latency is large.)
title: Timestepper refactor: simplifications, bug fixes
